title: Standard Monomials for Positroid Varieties
speaker: Daoji Huang
abstract:
Influential work of Hodge from the 1940s led the way in using Gröbner bases to combinatorially study the Grassmannian. We follow Hodge's approach to investigate certain subvarieties of the Grassmannian, called positroid varieties. Positroid varieties, introduced by Knutson--Lam--Speyer in 2013, provide a stratification of the Grassmannian which enjoys many advantages over other previously studied decompositions. In this talk, we present an explicit combinatorial description of the standard monomials and Gröbner bases of positroid varieties under the Hodge degeneration. As applications, we establish a connection between cyclic and reflectional symmetry of positroid varieties and well-known combinatorial operations on semistandard Young tableaux. We also give a formula for computing the characters of cyclic Demazure modules by a recurrence on the Hilbert series of positroid varieties, answering a question of Lam.
This is based on joint work with Ayah Almousa and Shiliang Gao.

proof_stages.label: Hodge standard monomial theory for the Grassmannian
proof_stages.summary: The talk recalls that Plücker straightening relations rewrite incomparable products, that reverse lexicographic order makes those products initial terms, and hence Hodge’s standard monomials for the Grassmannian are semistandard Young tableaux.

proof_stages.label: Positroid varieties and geometric input
proof_stages.summary: Positroid varieties are introduced as cyclic intersections of Schubert data, indexed by bounded affine permutations and cyclic rank conditions, with key facts that they are projections of Richardson varieties and are Frobenius split.

proof_stages.label: Reduction to basic interval rank conditions
proof_stages.summary: The speaker reduces the standard-monomial problem to a single non-wrapped interval rank condition, noting that general positroid varieties are obtained by intersections of basic ones and wrapped intervals are handled by passing to the dual Grassmannian.

proof_stages.label: Basic interval theorem via generalized antidiagonal avoidance
proof_stages.summary: For a basic interval positroid variety, the standard monomials are characterized as semistandard tableaux avoiding a generalized antidiagonal of length r+1 in the interval, and a Gröbner basis is obtained from Plücker relations plus straightening relations for minimal such antidiagonals.

proof_stages.label: Extension to arbitrary positroid varieties
proof_stages.summary: The basic Gröbner bases can be concatenated for intersections because Frobenius splitting prevents nonreduced behavior, yielding the standard-monomial description for any positroid variety.

proof_stages.label: Compatibility with promotion, rotation, evacuation, and duality
proof_stages.summary: After recalling jeu de taquin promotion and evacuation on rectangular semistandard tableaux, the talk states that cyclic rotation of positroid varieties corresponds to promotion of standard monomials, while Grassmannian duality corresponds to evacuation.

proof_stages.label: Hilbert-series and cyclic Demazure applications
proof_stages.summary: The standard monomial description gives multigraded Hilbert series for positroid varieties, leads to a recurrence via hyperplane sections and matrix Schubert varieties, and yields an inductive character formula for cyclic Demazure modules.
